title: Orange Loaf
excerpt: If you're not one for very sweet things, this loaf is for you. Nice and moist and fresh. If you have oranges and flour in your house, you can bake this. Lovely with coffee, tea, or as a dessert with custard. I got the recipe from my sister Jeanine …
html:
If you're not one for very sweet things, this loaf is for you. Nice and moist and fresh. If you have oranges and flour in your house, you can bake this. Lovely with coffee , tea or as a dessert with custard. I got the recipe from my sister Jeanine du Preez.

Ingredients

  • 1 large orange (juice and zest)
  • 3 large eggs
  • 150 g butter, melted
  • 160 g castor sugar
  • 160 g cake flour
  • 2,5 ml baking powder
  • 125 ml orange juice or orange liqueur to pour over the warm loaf afterwards

Method

  1. Preheat the oven to 180C
  2. Whisk the eggs and castor sugar until light and creamy.
  3. Sift in the flour and baking powder and stir it into the egg mixture.
  4. Stir in the melted butter.
  5. Now add the juice and finely grated zest of one fresh orange.
  6. Grease a loaf pan and pour the batter into the pan.
  7. Bake for 40 minutes
  8. Once the loaf is done and comes out of the oven, pour 125 ml orange juice or orange liqueur over the loaf. Let it cool in the pan.
